symptoms.symptom_name: Profound progressive fatigue and weakness over 3 months

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Chronic anaemia produces progressive fatigue; non-specific but consistent.
Profound exertional and rest fatigue indicates a significant fall in oxygen-carrying capacity.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recurrent syncope on standing (two episodes today)

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Symptomatic severe anaemia with orthostatic hypotension
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Recurrent orthostatic syncope marks haemodynamically significant anaemia and a high falls risk.
Recurrent syncope in an elderly patient mandates exclusion of a cardiac cause that cannot be excluded remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chronic poor dietary intake (tea and toast, no green vegetables)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A diet devoid of green leafy vegetables is a classic cause of dietary folate deficiency.
Glossitis is a recognised mucosal sign of megaloblastic (folate/B12) deficiency; partially video-observable but formal assessment needs examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: Angular stomatitis (cracks at the corners of the mouth)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Angular stomatitis accompanies nutritional deficiency states; non-specific.

symptoms.symptom_name: Absence of peripheral neuropathy / normal reported sensation in hands and feet

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Peripheral neuropathy and dorsal-column signs favour B12 deficiency; their absence points toward folate, but does not exclude B12.
Folate deficiency typically lacks the neurological features of B12 deficiency.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intact vibration/proprioception and negative Romberg on examination

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Preserved dorsal-column function argues against B12-related cord disease; requires neurological examination the AI cannot perform.

symptoms.symptom_name: Orthostatic blood-pressure drop 100/60 supine to 85/50 standing

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: Symptomatic severe anaemia with orthostatic hypotension
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective postural drop confirms haemodynamic compromise; a sealed clinician measurement the AI must not assume it has.

symptoms.symptom_name: Sinus tachycardia (HR 105) and soft systolic flow murmur

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Compensatory tachycardia and a high-output flow murmur reflect severe anaemia; require in-person measurement and auscultation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Mild scleral icterus and marked conjunctival/palmar pallor

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Pallor reflects profound anaemia; mild icterus reflects ineffective erythropoiesis in megaloblastic anaemia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Macrocytic anaemia with low serum/RBC folate and hypersegmented neutrophils on film

investigation_details.investigation_name: Full blood count, film and serum/RBC folate
investigation_details.expected_result: Raised MCV, low haemoglobin, low serum/RBC folate, hypersegmented neutrophils and macro-ovalocytes on film
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The cause and severity of the anaemia remain undefined; treatment cannot be safely directed.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Macrocytic anaemia with low folate and megaloblastic film changes defines the diagnosis — obtainable only in person.

investigation_details.expected_result: Normal (required to exclude co-existing B12 deficiency before folate replacement)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Folate given without excluding B12 deficiency can mask and worsen B12-related neurological disease.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal B12 is required before folate is started; the AI cannot obtain it, so it must not initiate folate.

symptom_clusters.cluster_name: Symptomatic severe anaemia with orthostatic compromise
symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Symptomatic severe anaemia with orthostatic hypotension
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together these define a haemodynamically significant anaemia requiring urgent in-person assessment, independent of the underlying nutritional cause.
Poor folate intake plus glossitis/angular stomatitis, absent neuropathy and macrocytic anaemia point to folate rather than B12 deficiency — but only pathology can confirm it.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Full blood count and blood film
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Defines the severity and morphology of the anaemia (macrocytosis, hypersegmented neutrophils).
Confirms folate deficiency as the cause.
Must be checked before folate replacement to avoid masking B12 deficiency.
Excludes a coexisting or alternative iron-deficiency contribution.
Screens for a cardiac/arrhythmic cause of recurrent syncope in an elderly patient.
Assess ineffective erythropoiesis / haemolysis contributing to the icterus.
